mission.title: Install and Configure Containerd on Kubernetes
mission.description: containerd is an industry-standard container runtime designed for simplicity, robustness, and portability. It manages the complete container lifecycle, making it essential for running containers efficiently in various environments.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Wiremind Helm repository to your local Helm setup to access the containerd-registry-config chart.
```bash
helm repo add wiremind https://wiremind.github.io/wiremind-helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install containerd using Helm
steps.description:
Install the containerd-registry-config chart in a new namespace called 'containerd'.
```bash
helm install containerd-registry-config wiremind/containerd-registry-config --namespace containerd --create-namespace --version 0.1.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the pods are running successfully in the 'containerd' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the deployment to set resource limits for the containerd pods.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment containerd-registry-config -n containerd
# Add resource limits under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: 500m
#     memory: 512Mi
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for security
steps.description:
Create a Role and RoleBinding to allow the containerd service account to access necessary resources.
```bash
kubectl create role containerd-role --verb=get,list,watch --resource=pods --namespace=containerd
kubectl create rolebinding containerd-rolebinding --role=containerd-role --serviceaccount=containerd:default --namespace=containerd
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the containerd-registry-config Helm release from the 'containerd' namespace.
```bash
helm uninstall containerd-registry-config --namespace containerd
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and other resources created during installation.
```bash
kubectl delete crd containerdregistryconfigs.wiremind.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'containerd' namespace to clean up all resources associated with the installation.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace containerd
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and state of the containerd installation.
```bash
kubectl get all -n containerd -o yaml > containerd-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure that you have the latest version of the Helm chart available.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Helm release
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the containerd-registry-config Helm release to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade containerd-registry-config wiremind/containerd-registry-config --namespace containerd --version 0.1.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the pods to ensure they are running the new version.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are not starting, check for resource availability in the cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n containerd
kubectl get nodes
```

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff error
troubleshooting.description:
If a pod is crashing, check the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n containerd
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter an error stating the Helm release is not found, ensure you are using the correct namespace.
```bash
helm list --namespace containerd
```

troubleshooting.title: RBAC permission errors
troubleshooting.description:
If you see permission denied errors, verify that the RBAC roles and bindings are correctly set up.
```bash
kubectl get rolebindings -n containerd
kubectl get roles -n containerd
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of containerd will result in running pods in the 'containerd' namespace, with all configurations applied. You can verify the installation by checking the status of the pods and ensuring they are in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add wiremind https://wiremind.github.io/wiremind-helm-charts
helm repo update

kubectl edit deployment containerd-registry-config -n containerd
# Add resource limits under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: 500m
#     memory: 512Mi

kubectl create role containerd-role --verb=get,list,watch --resource=pods --namespace=containerd
kubectl create rolebinding containerd-rolebinding --role=containerd-role --serviceaccount=containerd:default --namespace=containerd

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
